Explanation: Why vendor delivery totals do not equal output Volume
Formula / Notes: Different units of measure (one set or kg can produce many finished units), buffer stock from prior months is invisible here, MRO and packaging are not bill-of-materials inputs, deliveries are batched while output is daily. Vendor Deliveries is for SLA tracking, NOT material reconciliation.

Explanation: Why Daily Output Downtime_hrs does not equal the sum of Incidents Resolution_time_hrs
Formula / Notes: Downtime counts ALL line stops (planned maintenance, changeovers, breaks, incidents). Resolution counts only logged incidents from open to close. They overlap but are not equivalent.

Explanation: How partial vendor deliveries are recorded
Formula / Notes: Original date: row with Status = Partial and Lag_days reflecting when the backfill arrived. Backfill date: SEPARATE row with Status = Delivered and Notes = "Backfill of [date] shortfall". Examples: Delta Pratama May 14 → May 19 (300 units); Nusantara Parts May 13 → May 15 (2 units).

Explanation: Why the two percentages do not sum to 100
Formula / Notes: They measure different stages. Defects_pct = rejected at the QC gate. Yield_pct = good ÷ total processed. The gap between them is Scrap_units — material lost in-process before reaching QC. Three independent buckets.
